heading: Verification of compliance
label: 2577 Verification of compliance
notes: Amendments1999—Subsec. (a). , struck out “adequately” before “verified” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 106–113, § 1000(a)(7) [title XI, § 1115(a)]Subsecs. (b) to (d). , added subsec. (b) and redesignated former subsecs. (b) and (c) as (c) and (d), respectively. Language in section 1000(a)(7) [title XI, § 1115(b)] directing redesignation of subsec. (d) as (e) could not be executed. See 1998 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 106–113, § 1000(a)(7) [title XI, § 1115(b)]1998—Subsec. (a). , substituted “Secretary of State” for “Director” in introductory provisions and par. (1) and substituted “Department of State” for “Agency” in par. (3).Pub. L. 105–277, § 1223(11)(A)Subsec. (b). , substituted “Secretary of State” for “Director”.Pub. L. 105–277, § 1223(11)(A)Subsec. (d). , struck out heading and text of subsec. (d). Text read as follows: “In order to ensure adherence of the United States to obligations or commitments undertaken in arms control, nonproliferation, and disarmament agreements, and in order for the Director to make the assessment required by , the Director, or the Director’s designee, shall participate in all interagency groups or organizations within the executive branch of Government that assess, analyze, or review United States planned or ongoing policies, programs, or actions that have a direct bearing on United States adherence to obligations undertaken in arms control, nonproliferation, or disarmament agreements.”Pub. L. 105–277, § 1223(11)(B)section 2593a(a)(5) of this title1994— substituted “Verification of compliance” for “Verification of Arms Control Agreements” as section catchline and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text consisted of subsecs. (a) to (c) requiring the Director to report to Congress relating to verification of arms control proposals and agreements.Pub. L. 103–236

children.text: in the case of any arms control, nonproliferation, or disarmament agreement that has been concluded by the United States, the determination of the Secretary of State as to the degree to which the components of such agreement can be verified;

in the case of any arms control, nonproliferation, or disarmament agreement that has entered into force, any significant degradation or alteration in the capacity of the United States to verify compliance of the components of such agreement;
the amount and percentage of research funds expended by the Department of State for the purpose of analyzing issues relating to arms control, nonproliferation, and disarmament verification; and
the number of professional personnel assigned to arms control verification on a full-time basis by each Government agency.

children.heading: Assessments upon request
children.label: b Assessments upon request
children.text: Upon the request of the chairman or ranking minority member of the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate or the Committee on International Relations of the House of Representatives, in case of an arms control, nonproliferation, or disarmament proposal presented to a foreign country by the United States or presented to the United States by a foreign country, the Secretary of State shall submit a report to the Committee on the degree to which elements of the proposal are capable of being verified.

children.heading: Standard for verification of compliance
children.label: c Standard for verification of compliance
children.text: In making determinations under paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a), the Secretary of State shall assume that all measures of concealment not expressly prohibited could be employed and that standard practices could be altered so as to impede verification.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: d Rule of construction
children.text: Except as otherwise provided for by law, nothing in this section may be construed as requiring the disclosure of sensitive information relating to intelligence sources or methods or persons employed in the verification of compliance with arms control, nonproliferation, and disarmament agreements.
